request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the SEC approve a spot Bitcoin ETF by January 15, 2024?
Kind: retrospective
Timeline (5 events):
- 2024-01-01 [background] MSRB Update: Winter 2024 — MSRB winter update concerns municipal securities and general SEC matters; it gives no information affecting spot-Bitcoin ETF approval prospects.
- 2024-01-01 [background] 2011 - 2025 — Ghana SEC guidance on local custodians/fund managers is unrelated to the U.S. SEC's decision on U.S.-listed spot BTC ETFs, so no effect on YES probability.
- 2024-01-01 [background] 2025 Open Data Plan (PDF) — General SEC Open Data Plan unrelated to spot-BTC ETF approval; provides no info affecting likelihood of approval by Jan 15, 2024.
- 2023-12-31 [background] 2023 OIAD Annual Report (PDF) — A generic SEC annual report (OIAD) does not directly indicate approval or denial of a specific spot BTC ETF by Jan 15, 2024.
- 2023-12-13 [background] Standards for Covered Clearing Agencies for U.S. Treasury Securities and Application of the Broker-Dealer Customer Protection Rule With Respect to U.S. Treasury Securities Trading and Markets — SEC rulemaking on U.S. Treasury clearing and broker-dealer customer protection is unrelated to spot Bitcoin ETF approvals, so it doesn't change odds.

response.text: The listed SEC/MSRB documents in Dec 2023–Jan 1, 2024 were generic or unrelated to crypto and offered no signal on spot-Bitcoin ETF approval odds. On Jan 10, 2024, the SEC approved multiple spot Bitcoin ETF 19b-4s and let S-1s go effective; trading began Jan 11, resolving the market YES by Jan 15, 2024.
